section.title: Overview
section.para: Optional full-text-search / secondary-index companion to the cachedb_nats module. Loading this module is the enable switch: cachedb_nats binds it at startup, feeds the shared-memory field:value -> document keys index from its write path and KV-watcher process, and serves non-PK cache_query() / update() filters (including "search:term" raw queries) from it.

Without this module cachedb_nats accepts PK-only filters — the recommended shape for pure usrloc-as-store deployments, which then avoid the index shared memory, the watcher-feed CPU and the per-write lock traffic entirely.

section.title: index_buckets (integer)
section.para: Hash bucket count for the in-memory JSON-FTS search index. Higher values reduce average chain length on the lookup path at the cost of a larger fixed allocation for the bucket array (~32 KB per doubling).

The init code rounds the requested value up to the next power of two with a floor of 16 (NATS_IDX_SHARDS) so the hash function can use a bitmask AND instead of a modulo. Operators setting non-power-of-two values will see the rounded count logged at LM_INFO on startup.

Recommended values: 4096 at <= 20 000 AoRs, 16384 at 100 000, 65536 at 1 000 000. Above 1 000 000 endpoints the recommended path is to disable the index entirely via enable_search_index = 0; see the scaling notes in the opensips-usrloc-nats design repository for the analysis.

section.title: fts_max_results (integer)
section.para: The maximum number of documents returned by a single cache_query call. Limits memory consumption for broad queries that would otherwise match a large fraction of the index.
